title: UCP candidate will not sit in caucus if elected, after comments on transgender children: Smith
notes: Primary SAID source for EVT-114. Smith states Jennifer Johnson 'will not be sitting with the UCP caucus. And that is final.' Johnson had compared transgender children in schools to feces in a cookie-batter analogy in a September 2022 recording. No byline confirmed. URL needs HTTP check.
verification_notes: Verified 2026-06-18. Re EVT-114 (Johnson candidacy/2SLGBTQIA+ controversy). Article carries Johnson's walk-back ('concerned about life-altering medical procedures for children'; pledge to communicate concerns more respectfully) and Smith's statement that Johnson would not sit in caucus, calling the language 'offensive' and a 'vile analogy.' NDP (David Shepherd) called for dismissal citing the 'feces in cookie batter' analogy. Johnson ran in Lacombe-Ponoka. These quotes are candidate-refutation material for a future counter-memo.
